seo_title: ChainCaps: A Tool for Safe Agent Composition
meta_description: ChainCaps reduces attack success rate from 25-68% to 0-4.8% while preserving 96-100% benign completion in tool-using agents.
body_html:
ChainCaps addresses permission laundering by requiring every value to carry a sink-specific capability budget.

Key Benefits

  • Reduces attack success rate from 25-68% to 0-4.8%
  • Preserves 96-100% benign completion
  • Outperforms scalar-IFC and per-function-isolation baselines in replay experiments

Deployment Bottleneck

Manifest QualityAttack Blocking Rate
Expert manifests100%
Naive manifests27.3%

Implementation and Evaluation

ChainCaps is implemented as a transparent MCP proxy that requires no changes to the agent or tool servers.

In 82 tasks across five frontier models from three providers, ChainCaps reduces attack success rate while preserving benign completion.

source.title: ChainCaps: Composition-Safe Tool-Using Agents via Monotonic Capability Attenuation
source.summary: Researchers have developed ChainCaps, a tool that addresses the safety gap in tool composition by ensuring that values carry a sink-specific capability budget. This allows ChainCaps to detect potential security threats and prevent them from occurring. In experiments across five frontier models, ChainCaps reduced attack success rates from 25-68% to 0-4.8%, while preserving benign completion rates of 96-100%. The tool's effectiveness is limited to explicit-flow composition safety under trusted manifests and proxy-visible data movement.
